content_en:
The Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra is organized according to the classical tabaqat (generations/classes) method that was the standard framework for Islamic biographical literature. The fundamental organizing principle is chronological: scholars are grouped by the generation in which they lived and worked, beginning with the Companions and Followers who transmitted from the Prophet and proceeding generation by generation through the centuries.

Within each generation, Taj ad-Din includes all scholars who identified with or contributed significantly to the Shafi'i legal tradition. His criteria for inclusion are broad — he includes not only jurists in the strict sense but also hadith scholars, Qur'anic commentators, Sufi masters, and theologians who worked within a broadly Shafi'i framework. This comprehensive approach makes the Tabaqat al-Kubra (the "Great Tabaqat") considerably more extensive than earlier works in the same genre.

For each scholar, Taj ad-Din follows a standard biographical template: he records the scholar's full name and genealogy (nasab), his teachers and the scholars from whom he received ijazah (authorization to transmit), his students and those he taught, significant positions he held, important works he authored, noteworthy scholarly opinions or legal rulings he issued, and the date and circumstances of his death. The length of individual entries varies enormously — major figures like Al-Nawawi receive extensive treatment spanning many pages, while minor figures receive only brief notices.

A distinctive feature of the work is Taj ad-Din's inclusion of substantial quotations from primary sources. Rather than merely summarizing what others said, he often quotes at length from the works and recorded statements of the scholars he treats, giving readers direct access to their thought. He also quotes extensively from earlier biographical works, making the Tabaqat al-Kubra a valuable repository of information from sources that have sometimes been lost.

Taj ad-Din exercises critical judgment throughout. He does not merely repeat whatever he has read but evaluates sources, notes contradictions in the record, and sometimes defends scholars against unfair characterizations. His assessments of scholars' positions within internal Shafi'i debates show sophisticated knowledge of the school's jurisprudential history.

The work is also distinguished by the extensive theological introductions and discussions Taj ad-Din inserts at key points. He uses the occasion of writing a biographical dictionary to address major theological and methodological questions facing the Shafi'i school, particularly regarding the relationship between jurisprudence and Ash'ari theology. This makes the Tabaqat al-Kubra not merely a reference work but a substantive intellectual contribution to Shafi'i self-understanding.
content_id:
Thabaqat asy-Syafi'iyyah al-Kubra diorganisasi menurut metode thabaqat (generasi/kelas) klasik yang merupakan kerangka standar bagi literatur biografi Islam. Prinsip pengorganisasian fundamental adalah kronologis: para ulama dikelompokkan berdasarkan generasi tempat mereka hidup dan bekerja, dimulai dari para Sahabat dan Tabi'in yang meriwayatkan dari Nabi dan berlanjut generasi demi generasi sepanjang abad-abad.

Dalam setiap generasi, Taj ad-Din mencakup semua ulama yang mengidentifikasi diri dengan atau berkontribusi secara signifikan pada tradisi hukum Syafi'i. Kriteria penerimaannya luas. Ia mencakup bukan hanya para ahli fikih dalam pengertian ketat, tetapi juga para ulama hadis, para mufassir, para syaikh sufi, dan para teolog yang bekerja dalam kerangka Syafi'i yang lebih luas. Pendekatan komprehensif ini menjadikan Thabaqat al-Kubra jauh lebih luas daripada karya-karya sebelumnya dalam genre yang sama.

Untuk setiap ulama, Taj ad-Din mengikuti template biografi standar: ia mencatat nama lengkap dan silsilah ulama tersebut (nasab), guru-gurunya dan ulama-ulama dari siapa ia menerima ijazah (izin untuk meriwayatkan), murid-muridnya dan mereka yang ia ajar, jabatan-jabatan signifikan yang ia pegang, karya-karya penting yang ia karang, pendapat-pendapat ilmiah atau fatwa-fatwa yang patut diperhatikan yang ia keluarkan, serta tanggal dan keadaan wafatnya. Panjang entri-entri individual sangat bervariasi: tokoh-tokoh besar seperti an-Nawawi menerima perlakuan ekstensif yang mencakup banyak halaman, sementara tokoh-tokoh minor hanya mendapat catatan singkat.

Ciri khas karya ini adalah penyertaan Taj ad-Din atas kutipan-kutipan substansial dari sumber-sumber primer. Alih-alih sekadar meringkas apa yang dikatakan orang lain, ia sering mengutip secara panjang lebar dari karya-karya dan pernyataan-pernyataan yang tercatat dari para ulama yang ia bahas, memberikan pembaca akses langsung ke pemikiran mereka. Ia juga mengutip secara ekstensif dari karya-karya biografi sebelumnya, menjadikan Thabaqat al-Kubra repositori berharga informasi dari sumber-sumber yang terkadang telah hilang.

Taj ad-Din menerapkan penilaian kritis di sepanjang karya. Ia tidak sekadar mengulangi apa yang ia baca, tetapi mengevaluasi sumber-sumber, mencatat kontradiksi-kontradiksi dalam rekam jejak, dan terkadang membela ulama-ulama terhadap karakterisasi-karakterisasi yang tidak adil. Penilaian-penilaiannya atas posisi-posisi ulama dalam perdebatan-perdebatan internal Syafi'i menunjukkan pengetahuan yang canggih tentang sejarah yurisprudensial mazhab tersebut.

Karya ini juga dibedakan oleh mukadimah-mukadimah dan diskusi-diskusi teologis yang ekstensif yang Taj ad-Din sisipkan pada titik-titik kunci. Ia menggunakan kesempatan penulisan kamus biografi untuk membahas pertanyaan-pertanyaan aqidah dan metodologis besar yang dihadapi mazhab Syafi'i, terutama mengenai hubungan antara fikih dan aqidah Asy'ariyah. Hal ini menjadikan Thabaqat al-Kubra bukan sekadar karya referensi, tetapi kontribusi intelektual substantif bagi pemahaman diri Syafi'i.
content_ar: The Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra is organized وفقاً لـ the الكلاسيكي tabaqat (generations/classes) method that was the standard framework for Islamic biographical literature. The الأساسي organizing principle is chronological: العلماء are grouped by the generation in which they lived and worked, beginning with the الصحابة and Followers who transmitted from النبي ﷺ and proceeding generation by generation through the centuries. Within each generation, Taj ad-Din includes all العلماء who identified with or contributed significantly to the الشافعي legal التراث. His criteria for inclusion are broad — he includes not only jurists in the strict sense but أيضاً الحديث العلماء, Qur'anic commentators, Sufi masters, and theologians who worked within a broadly الشافعي framework. This comprehensive approach makes the Tabaqat al-Kubra (the "Great Tabaqat") considerably more extensive than earlier works in the same genre. For each scholar, Taj ad-Din follows a standard biographical template: he records the scholar's full name and genealogy (nasab), his teachers and the العلماء from whom he received ijazah (authorization to transmit), his students and those he taught, المهم positions he held, المهم works ألّف, noteworthy العلمي opinions or legal rulings he issued, and the date and circumstances of his الموت. The length of individual entries varies enormously — major figures like النووي receive extensive treatment spanning many pages, بينما minor figures receive only brief notices. A distinctive feature of the work is Taj ad-Din's inclusion of substantial quotations from الأساسي sources. Rather than merely summarizing what others said, he often quotes at length from the works and recorded statements of the العلماء he treats, giving readers direct access to their thought. He أيضاً quotes extensively from earlier biographical works, making the Tabaqat al-Kubra a valuable repository of information from sources that have sometimes been lost. Taj ad-Din exercises البالغ الأهمية judgment throughout. He does not merely repeat whatever he has read but evaluates sources, notes contradictions in the record, and sometimes defends العلماء against unfair characterizations. His assessments of العلماء' positions within internal الشافعي debates show sophisticated العلم of the school's jurisprudential history. The work is أيضاً distinguished by the extensive theological introductions and discussions Taj ad-Din inserts at الرئيسي points. He uses the occasion of writing a biographical dictionary to address major theological and methodological questions facing the الشافعي school, particularly فيما يخص the relationship between jurisprudence and الأشعري theology. This makes the Tabaqat al-Kubra not merely a reference work but a substantive intellectual contribution to الشافعي self-الفهم.
